summary: Full backend + frontend testing for Fernanda Caldeira Advocacia landing page. 7/7 pytest backend tests passed (root welcome, POST /api/contact valid+validation, GET /api/contact list w/ persistence and ordering). Frontend Playwright flow: hero heading present, nav visible, all 6 WhatsApp buttons have valid wa.me/5512982654821 hrefs, Google Maps embed present, FAQ accordion expands (aria-expanded=true), nav anchor scroll works (scrollY 2336), contact form success flow (toast 'Mensagem enviada' + form cleared), empty-form validation toast shown, mobile menu open/close works on 390px viewport.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py stores created_at as ISO string on insert but re-parses to datetime in list response — works, but consider storing as native BSON datetime for proper server-side sort by date type (currently sort works because ISO 8601 UTC strings are lexicographically sortable).
Contact model uses EmailStr import but ContactLeadCreate.email is a plain str; import is unused and email is not validated. Low priority (email is optional).
CORS is wide-open ('*') which is fine for a public landing page but consider restricting for production.
No rate limiting on POST /api/contact — public endpoint could be spam-abused. Consider adding basic throttling.
GET /api/contact is publicly accessible and returns all leads with PII (name/phone/email/message). This should be admin-only in production.
seed_data_creation: Created a handful of TEST_-prefixed contact leads in MongoDB via API during testing (not cleaned up; harmless as this is fresh dev DB).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py; run via `pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Public URL used: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Contact form submit is instant (~500ms) — no long AI waits. Mobile menu uses framer AnimatePresence so element is removed from DOM on close (assert on count()==0).
